text: Today, we're going to review our pubic cervical fascia reconstruction and placation. This is a step that we incorporate into the robotic sacrococopexy in those patients that has an apical and anterior compartment defect.

The surgeon uses a prograsper to release tension proximally, adjusting the tissue to achieve the desired tension for placation.
The surgeon performs seven to twelve placations, moving from left to right and distal to proximal, adjusting the technique based on the patient's anatomy.

And I think just by reinforcing this, we may prevent, as we have seen in the short-term follow-up, of course, these anterior compartment failures that we see in some of our patients as soon as three or six months.
